title: The Last Signal
notes: Post-collapse sci-fi; high-tension opening at remote radio tower; duo survivors with contrasting skills (Mara: tech, Rafi: medic); resource scarcity stakes; subtle worldbuilding through relics and broadcasts; mid-film detour to barter outpost; third-act set piece in derelict relay; trust vs. paranoia theme; focus on character choices; textured sound design; leave twist of the final transmission out of review; note the cinematography during night sequences; synth-heavy original score; restrained runtime; potential streaming release window mentioned in trade reports

title: Whispers in the Fog
notes: Gothic coastal mystery; small harbor town with lighthouse; missing-person case as inciting incident; fog motifs and creaking boats; mood-first pacing (slow burn); intimate, character-driven relationships; standout performance from lead detective; layered production design in old boathouse; major reveal happens at the cliffs (do not spoil); praise the cinematography and use of natural light; sparse, eerie original score; thematically about secrets and community guilt

title: Starbound: Europa
notes: Near-future expedition under Europa's ice; claustrophobic habitat corridors; team dynamics under pressure; practical effects with smart VFX assists; under-ice drone sequences; science jargon kept approachable; one nail-biter EVA set piece; avoid revealing what is found below the ice; tone: grounded sci-fi thriller; mention mission logs structure; strong original score with analog textures; theatrical run rumored, possible streaming release later
